seo.title: Building Workflow Automation with AutoGen + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build workflow automation with AutoGen + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building Workflow Automation with AutoGen enables multi-agent systems to handle complex, multi-step tasks with API integrations, conditional logic, and human approvals. However, conversational agents risk infinite loops, unsafe code execution, and incorrect decisions that can cascade through workflows. Vex provides essential guardrails by filtering agent replies to ensure action correctness, permission validation, and safe execution.

architectureOverview.heading: Workflow Automation Architecture with AutoGen
architectureOverview.description:
The architecture features a Supervisor Agent that orchestrates task execution by delegating to specialized Worker Agents (API Caller, Validator, Condition Checker). Each agent collaborates conversationally, with human-in-the-loop approvals at critical decision points. The workflow processes sequential steps like data retrieval, validation, conditional branching, and final execution, all while maintaining conversation state.

API integrations occur through code-executing agents that make HTTP calls or database queries. Conditional logic is handled by decision agents that analyze prior outputs. Human approvals are triggered via registered functions that pause execution for manual review. Vex integrates as a reply filter to validate every agent response before it propagates through the workflow.

This setup scales to complex enterprise workflows but amplifies risks from agent miscommunication or hallucinated actions.

Supervisor Agent

   +---+---+
   |       |
Worker  Human
Agents  Approval
(API/   (oobabooga

    Vex Filter
(Reply  |
Validation)   --> Final Action

risks.name: Infinite conversation loops between agents
risks.description: Agents enter repetitive back-and-forth without task progression
risks.mitigation: Vex conversation state tracking and loop detection

risks.name: Code execution producing unsafe outputs
risks.description: API agents execute dangerous code or make unauthorized calls
risks.mitigation: Vex permission validation and sandboxed execution checks

risks.name: Agents agreeing on incorrect conclusions
risks.description: Multiple agents validate wrong actions through group hallucination
risks.mitigation: Vex action correctness verification against workflow spec

risks.name: Uncontrolled token spending in multi-turn debates
risks.mitigation: Vex token budget enforcement and early termination

implementation.title: Set up Multi-Agent Workflow GroupChat
implementation.description: Create supervisor and worker agents with group chat manager for workflow orchestration
implementation.code:
from autogen import AssistantAgent, GroupChat, GroupChatManager

supervisor = AssistantAgent(
    name="Supervisor",
    llm_config={"config_list": [{"model": "gpt-4", "api_key": "your_key"}]},
    system_message="Orchestrate workflow: delegate to API/Validator agents"
)

api_agent = AssistantAgent(
    name="API_Agent",
    llm_config={"config_list": [{"model": "gpt-4", "api_key": "your_key"}]},
    system_message="Execute safe API calls only"
)

groupchat = GroupChat(agents=[supervisor, api_agent], messages=[])
manager = GroupChatManager(groupchat=groupchat)

implementation.title: Implement Human-in-the-Loop Approval
implementation.description: Register approval function for critical decision points
implementation.code:
def human_approval(context, sender):
    action = context.get('proposed_action')
    print(f"Approve? {action}")
    return input("y/n: ") == 'y'

api_agent.register_for_llm(name="approval", description="Request human approval")(human_approval)

# Trigger in workflow
supervisor.initiate_chat(
    api_agent,
    message="Execute payment workflow - check approvals first"
)

vexIntegration.title: Register Vex as Reply Function Filter
vexIntegration.description: Install and configure Vex to filter all agent replies for safety
vexIntegration.code:
pip install autogen vex-sdk

from vex_sdk import VexClient
from autogen import register_function

vex = VexClient(api_key="your_vex_key")

def vex_reply_filter(context, sender):
    message = sender.last_message()['content']
    verdict = vex.verify({
        'action': 'agent_reply',
        'content': message,
        'workflow_step': context.get('step'),
        'permissions': ['read_api', 'write_safe']
    })
    return verdict['approved']

# Register globally
register_function(
    vex_reply_filter,
    caller=api_agent,
    executor=supervisor,
    name="vex_filter",
    description="Vex safety filter"
)
